channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mega Millions winner sues for other half of $394M jackpot, claims he bought both tickets
item.description: A man is suing the California Lottery, claiming he bought both winning tickets for a $394 million Mega Millions jackpot but lost one and is owed the remaining $197 million.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: San Diego raises bar to work with immigration officials ahead of Trump's deportation efforts
item.description: The nation’s fifth most populous county has decided to limit cooperation with federal immigration authorities beyond what California law dictates, San Diego County on the border with Mexico is allying itself with jurisdictions across the country that are raising new obstacles to President-elect Dona
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Joe Burrow's home broken into during Monday Night Football
item.description: Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into during Monday Night Football in the latest home invasion of a pro athlete in the U.S., authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: After acquittal in subway chokehold trial, Daniel Penny says he was vulnerable in the encounter
item.description: The Marine veteran who choked a volatile, mentally ill man on a New York subway says he put himself in a “very vulnerable position.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The 26-year-old man charged in last week’s killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO appeared in a Pennsylvania courtroom Tuesday, where he was denied bail and his lawyer said he'd fight extradition to New York City, where the attack happened. Luigi Nicholas Mangione was arrested Monday in the Dec.
item.dc:creator: SARAH BRUMFIELD and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KTLA 5

item.title: Oregon federal judge temporarily blocks proposed Kroger-Albertsons merger
item.description: Oregon was one of several states to join the Federal Trade Commission’s lawsuit that aimed to block the merger.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pepperdine student feared for his life amid Franklin Fire
item.description: Shelter-in-place orders have since been lifted with students either choosing to leave campus, return to their dorms or remain in the library.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Monarch butterflies to be listed as a threatened species in US
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — U.S. wildlife officials announced a decision Tuesday to extend federal protections to monarch butterflies after years of warnings from environmentalists that populations are shrinking and the beloved pollinator may not survive climate change. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: From wealth and success to murder suspect, the life of Luigi Mangione took a hard turn
item.description: Luigi Nicholas Mangione, the suspect in the fatal shooting of a healthcare executive in New York City, apparently was living a charmed life: the grandson of a wealthy real estate developer, valedictorian of his elite Baltimore prep school and with degrees from one of the nation's top private univers
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect has outburst outside Pennsylvania courthouse
item.description: Luigi Mangione shouted and struggled against escorting officers as he was led into court on Tuesday, where he was denied bail; his attorney intends to file a writ of habeas corpus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US wildlife officials plan to label monarch butterflies as threatened species
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service plans to add the butterfly to the threatened species list by the end of next year following an extensive public comment period.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McConnell receives medical attention after falling
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) received medical attention after falling following a Republican lunch meeting Tuesday. A spokesman for McConnell said the GOP leader tripped after the lunch. "He sustained a minor cut to the face and sprained his wrist.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Susan Wild absent from Ethics Committee meeting after Gaetz leaks to press
item.description: Rep. Susan Wild (Pa.), the top Democrat on the House Ethics Committee, was absent from the panel’s meeting last week after being traced as the source of leaks to the press regarding the investigation into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Santa’s home is on Zillow, and you can take an AI-powered tour
item.description: You can take an AI-powered tour of Santa’s home, and you can even decorate your home like Santa with these festive décor ideas.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Descendant of last native leader of Alaska island demands Japanese reparations for 1942 invasion
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Helena Pagano's great-grandfather was the last Alaska Native chief of a remote island in the Bering Sea, closer to Russia than North America.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of suspect speaks out after his arrest in UnitedHealthcare CEO's murder
item.description: The family of Luigi Mangione, the 26-year-old man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in New York City, has released a statement following his arrest on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Family of Luigi Mangione speaks out following arrest and arraignment
item.description: The family of Luigi Mangione, the 26-year-old man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in New York City, has released a statement following his arrest on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Polygamous sect leader gets 50 years in prison in scheme to orchestrate sex involving children
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A polygamist religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives” including 10 underage girls was sentenced to 50 years in prison on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults, and for scheming to kidnap them from
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Local Pennsylvania officer hailed hero for 'swift' actions in arrest of UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect
item.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro praised Altoona Police Department Office Tyler Frye for his swift actions in arresting UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect Luigi Mangione.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about Luigi Mangione, the suspect arrested in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, ABC 4

item.title: How to watch 'Monday Night Football' in 'The Simpsons' universe
item.description: The prime-time matchup between the Cincinnati Bengals and Dallas Cowboys will also take place at Springfield’s Atoms Stadium as part of “The Simpsons Funday Football” alternate broadcast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny is acquitted in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Daniel Penny acquitted in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Passenger aboard Volaris flight in Mexico arrested after allegedly trying to divert plane to US
item.description: A passenger aboard a domestic Volaris flight in Mexico was arrested after he allegedly tried to divert the plane to the U.S., the airline said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok asks federal appeals court to bar enforcement of potential ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: TikTok asked a federal appeals court on Monday to bar the Biden administration from enforcing a law that could lead to a ban on the popular platform until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge to the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing charged with murder, court records show
item.description: A man in Pennsylvania has been arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KRON4

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Juan Soto agrees to record $765 million, 15-year contract with Mets, AP source says
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Star outfielder Juan Soto and the New York Mets agreed Sunday to a record $765 million, 15-year contract, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press, a deal that could escalate to $805 million and is believed to be the largest pact in team sports history.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Netflix must pay $385K to woman exposed as fertility doctor's secret child: court
item.description: A federal court in Indianapolis has ordered Netflix to pay $385,000 to an Indiana woman it outed as the daughter of Donald Cline, a former fertility doctor who secretly used his own sperm to inseminate patients.
item.dc:creator: Austin Hanson, KTLA 5

item.title: Allen sets NFL mark with 3 TD passes and 3 TD runs, but Stafford's Rams hold off Bills 44-42
item.description: Matthew Stafford and the Los Angeles Rams had to pile up a veritable mountain of points, yards and time-of-possession advantage early on to give themselves even a chance against the Buffalo Bills' ferocious fourth-quarter rally and a historic six-touchdown performance by Josh Allen.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A timeline of the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson and search for his killer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The search for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson ’s killer since Thompson was ambushed Wednesday outside a Midtown Manhattan hotel has stretched beyond New York City.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A hamstring injury silenced George Pickens. His Steelers teammates picked up the slack vs. Browns
item.description: Pickens missed the first game of his career on Sunday, watching from the sideline in Pittsburgh's 27-14 win over Cleveland that showcased the team's receivers group runs deeper than the unit's talented if occasionally petulant star.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Darnold delivers for Vikings with career-high 347 yards and 5 TDs to beat Falcons, Cousins 42-21
item.description: Darnold added yet another highlight to his brilliant first season with the Vikings, passing for 347 yards and five touchdowns, both career highs, in a 42-21 victory over the foundering Falcons on Sunday to stretch their winning streak to six games.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 10 hospitalized after motorcycle officer crashes into crowd at California parade
item.description: A crash involving a motorcycle officer left at least 10 people hospitalized during a holiday parade in Palm Springs, California, Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Saints QB Derek Carr injures left hand on dive in 4th quarter of win over Giants
item.description: New Orleans Saints quarterback Derek Carr injured his left hand late in the fourth quarter of Sunday's 14-11 victory over the New York Giants when he went airborne while trying for a first down and crashed to the turf.
item.dc:creator: TOM CANAVAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9
